title: श्रीहरि स्तुति
devanagariText:
॥ श्रीहरि स्तुति ॥

श्रियः कान्ताय कल्याणनिधये निधयेऽर्थिनाम्।
श्रीवेङ्कटनिवासाय श्रीनिवासाय मंगलम्॥ १॥

नारायण नारायण जय गोविन्द हरे।
नारायण नारायण जय गोपाल हरे॥ २॥

सर्वधर्मान्परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज।
अहं त्वां सर्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः॥ ३॥

हरिर्दाता हरिर्भोक्ता हरिरन्नं प्रजापतिः।
हरिः सर्वशरीरस्थो भुंक्ते भोजयते हरिः॥ ४॥

सर्वे भवन्तु सुखिनः सर्वे सन्तु निरामयाः।
सर्वे भद्राणि पश्यन्तु मा कश्चिद्दुःखभाग्भवेत्॥ ५॥

वासुदेवसुतं देवं कंसचाणूरमर्दनम्।
देवकीपरमानन्दं कृष्णं वन्दे जगद्गुरुम्॥ ६॥

कृष्णाय वासुदेवाय हरये परमात्मने।
प्रणतक्लेशनाशाय गोविन्दाय नमो नमः॥ ७॥

ॐ नमो भगवते वासुदेवाय नमः।
ॐ नमो नारायणाय नमः।
हरे कृष्ण हरे कृष्ण कृष्ण कृष्ण हरे हरे।
हरे राम हरे राम राम राम हरे हरे॥ ८॥

॥ इति श्रीहरि स्तुति सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Hari Stuti ||

Shriyah Kantaya Kalyana Nidhaye Nidhaye'rthinam |
Shri Venkata Nivasaya Shri Nivasaya Mangalam || 1 ||

Narayana Narayana Jaya Govinda Hare |
Narayana Narayana Jaya Gopala Hare || 2 ||

Sarva Dharman Parityajya Mam Ekam Sharanam Vraja |
Aham Tvam Sarva Papebhyo Mokshayishyami Ma Shuchah || 3 ||

Harir Data Harir Bhokta Harir Annam Prajapatih |
Harih Sarva Sharira Stho Bhunkte Bhojayate Harih || 4 ||

Sarve Bhavantu Sukhinah Sarve Santu Niramayah |
Sarve Bhadrani Pashyantu Ma Kashchid Duhkhabhag Bhavet || 5 ||

Vasudevasutam Devam Kamsa Chanura Mardanam |
Devaki Paramanandaam Krishnam Vande Jagadgurum || 6 ||

Krishnaya Vasudevaya Haraye Paramatmane |
Pranata Klesha Nashaya Govindaya Namo Namah || 7 ||

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Namah |
Om Namo Narayanaya Namah |
Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare |
Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare || 8 ||

|| Iti Shri Hari Stuti Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
श्री (लक्ष्मी) के कान्त, कल्याणनिधि, याचकों के निधि, श्रीवेंकट में निवास करने वाले श्रीनिवास को मंगल हो।

नारायण नारायण! जय गोविन्द हरे! जय गोपाल हरे!

(गीता १८.६६) सब धर्मों को त्यागकर मेरी एक शरण में आओ। मैं तुम्हें सब पापों से मुक्त करूंगा, शोक मत करो।

हरि ही दाता हैं, हरि ही भोक्ता हैं, हरि ही अन्न हैं, हरि ही प्रजापति हैं। हरि सबके शरीर में स्थित हैं — हरि ही खाते हैं और खिलाते हैं।

सब सुखी हों, सब निरोग हों, सब कल्याण देखें, कोई दुःख का भागी न हो।

वासुदेव के पुत्र, कंस-चाणूर को मारने वाले, देवकी के परमानन्द, जगद्गुरु कृष्ण को वन्दन।

कृष्ण, वासुदेव, हरि, परमात्मा, शरणागतों के क्लेश नाश करने वाले गोविन्द को बारम्बार नमस्कार।

हरे कृष्ण हरे कृष्ण कृष्ण कृष्ण हरे हरे। हरे राम हरे राम राम राम हरे हरे।

Contains Bhagavad Gita's Charama Shloka (18.66) — supreme surrender verse
Includes the universal prayer Sarve Bhavantu Sukhinah
Ends with Hare Krishna Maha Mantra for nama sankeertana
Perfect for daily devotional recitation by all Vaishnavas
description: Shri Hari Stuti (श्रीहरि स्तुति) is a devotional hymn of praise dedicated to Lord Vishnu. This sacred composition is sourced from Bhagavad Gita / Vishnu Purana / Vaishnava Tradition and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to universal Hari stuti combining the most beloved Vishnu/Krishna verses as well as end with Hare Krishna Maha Mantra for nama sankeertana. Additionally, it is known to contain Bhagavad Gita's Charama Shloka (18.66) — supreme surrender verse. For best results, devotees chant this on Daily and during Janmashtami, Vaikuntha Ekadashi, and Tulsi Vivah. The text contains 8 verses and takes around 4 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Shri Hari Stuti in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Shri Hari Stuti and which Vishnu verses does it combine?
seoFaqs.answer: Shri Hari Stuti (श्रीहरि स्तुति) is a compact Vaishnava devotional anthology of eight verses curated to praise Lord Vishnu (Hari) using some of the most loved shlokas from the Bhagavad Gita and the wider Vaishnava tradition. It opens with the famous Shriyah Kantaya Kalyana-Nidhaye mangalam to Sri Venkateshwara, moves through the Narayana-Govinda-Hare nama-sankirtana, the Bhagavad Gita Charama Shloka (18.66 — Sarva-dharman parityajya), the Harir Data Harir Bhokta Gita verse, the universal peace-prayer Sarve bhavantu sukhinah, and concludes with the Hare Krishna Mahamantra. It is preserved in classical Vaishnava recitation tradition as a daily Vishnu praise composite. (Sources: Bhagavad Gita; Vishnu Purana; Vaishnava recitation tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of the Charama Shloka Sarva-dharman parityajya included in this stuti?
seoFaqs.answer: Sarva-dharman parityajya mam ekam sharanam vraja, aham tvam sarva-papebhyo mokshayishyami ma shuchah is Bhagavad Gita 18.66, the Charama Shloka or final teaching of Lord Krishna to Arjuna. It instructs the devotee to abandon all dharmas (rule-based righteousnesses) and take refuge in him alone, with the assurance that he will liberate the devotee from all sins, asking him not to grieve. Sri Vaishnava acharyas (especially Ramanuja in his Gita Bhashya) treat this verse as the supreme statement of saranagati (loving surrender) and the doctrinal heart of prapatti, which is why it is placed in the Hari Stuti as the philosophical core of the text. (Sources: Bhagavad Gita 18.66; Ramanuja Gita Bhashya.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Sarve Bhavantu Sukhinah in the Shri Hari Stuti?
seoFaqs.answer: Sarve bhavantu sukhinah, sarve santu niramayah, sarve bhadrani pashyantu, ma kashchid duhkha-bhag bhavet is one of the best-loved universal peace-prayers in the Hindu tradition, often classified as a Brihadaranyaka or general Vedic shanti-mantra. It prays: may all be happy, may all be free from disease, may all see auspiciousness, may none suffer sorrow. Its inclusion in the Shri Hari Stuti reflects the Vaishnava conviction that devotion to Hari naturally expands into universal compassion — bhakti is not an isolated salvation but a prayer for the welfare of all beings, including non-human creation. (Sources: Vedic shanti-mantra tradition; Vaishnava bhakti commentaries.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Shri Hari Stuti be recited and on which days?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shri Hari Stuti is suitable for daily recitation in the morning Brahma muhurta or after the morning bath, since it is a short composite (about four minutes) and includes the Mahamantra. It is especially auspicious on Thursdays (Guruvar — sacred to Vishnu and Brihaspati), Saturdays (in Ayyappa and Venkateshwara traditions Saturday is also Vishnu-day), and Ekadashi tithis. Major occasions for special recitation include Janmashtami (midnight birth of Krishna), Vaikuntha Ekadashi (the gateway of liberation), Tulsi Vivah, Vamana Jayanti, Narasimha Jayanti, Rama Navami and the four Chaturmasya months. It is also recited at gruha-pravesha, satyanarayana puja and any Vishnu archana. (Sources: Vaishnava panchanga tradition; standard nitya-paath manuals.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Hare Krishna Mahamantra and why is it placed at the end of this stuti?
seoFaqs.answer: The Hare Krishna Mahamantra — Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare, Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare — is a sixteen-name, thirty-two syllable mantra preserved in the Kali-Santarana Upanishad and declared by the Upanishad to be the prescribed remedy for the ills of the Kali Yuga: iti shodashakam namnam Kali-kalmasha-nashanam. Its placement as the final verse of the Shri Hari Stuti embodies the Vaishnava teaching that all praise of Hari culminates in nama-sankirtana — the loud and loving chanting of the holy name — which the Bhagavata declares to be more potent than yajna, tapas and tirtha-yatra in the present age. (Sources: Kali-Santarana Upanishad; Bhagavata Purana 12.3.51-52.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Sri Venkateshwara invoked in the opening Shriyah Kantaya verse?
seoFaqs.answer: The opening verse Shriyah Kantaya Kalyana-Nidhaye Nidhaye'rthinam, Shri Venkata Nivasaya Shri Nivasaya Mangalam is the celebrated mangala-shloka addressed to Lord Venkateshwara of Tirumala — the consort of Sri Lakshmi, treasure of auspiciousness, treasure of those who seek refuge, residing on the Venkata hill, and whose other name Shrinivasa means 'the abode of Sri'. Although the verse is famously associated with the daily Suprabhatam tradition of Tirumala (composed by Prativadi Bhayankaram Annangaracharya in the 15th century), it stands on its own as the auspicious opening to any Vishnu invocation. Its inclusion in the Hari Stuti links the daily devotee directly to the living Tirumala tradition. (Sources: Sri Venkateshwara Suprabhatam tradition; Annangaracharya compositions.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Shri Hari Stuti bestow according to tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: Although the Shri Hari Stuti as a composite has no single phalashruti, the individual verses carry powerful traditional fruits which are inherited by the composite. The Charama Shloka grants moksha through saranagati; the Sarve bhavantu sukhinah verse cultivates universal compassion and karuna; the Harir Data Harir Bhokta verse establishes Hari as the inner enjoyer of all yajna and food (key to Vaishnava bhojana-mantra); the Krishnam vande verses grant Krishna-bhakti; and the closing Mahamantra grants Kali-kalmasha-nashana — destruction of the sins specific to this age. Cumulatively the Hari Stuti is recited for daily refuge, peace of mind, removal of obstacles, family welfare, devotional growth and the merit of universal goodwill. (Sources: classical phalashruti traditions; Vaishnava commentaries.)
